2606.15191.title: AmchiBias: Measuring Stereotypical Bias in Goan Identity Groups with a Minimal Pair Dataset in English and Konkani
2606.15191.paper_summary: Socio-cultural stereotypical bias is an important consideration in the development and deployment of NLP systems. It is however often considered only at the national level, despite rich subnational socio-cultural structures. We present AmchiBias, the first benchmark for measuring socio-cultural stereotypical bias for the Indian state of Goa with its unique historically multicultural setting. It covers various Goan identity groups and comprises 313 minimal pairs across eight sociodemographic dimensions in both English and Devanagari Konkani. We then evaluate stereotypical bias in five multilingual encoder models on this benchmark. We find near-chance scores in Konkani, reflecting language incompetence for general multilingual models and a lack of Goan cultural competence for Indian language models. Queried in English, models with a stronger Indian language coverage show higher bias for pan-Indian groups than hyperlocal Goan groups. This suggests the English signal reflects pan-Indian pretraining associations rather than genuine Goan cultural knowledge. Our findings highlight a critical gap in low-resource multilingual NLP evaluation for hyperlocal community identities.
2606.15191.paper_summary_zh: 社會文化刻板印象偏見在自然語言處理系統的開發和部署中是一個重要的考量。 然而，這通常僅在國家層面上考慮，儘管存在豐富的次國家社會文化結構。 我們提出了AmchiBias，這是第一個用於測量印度果阿邦社會文化刻板印象偏見的基準，該地區擁有獨特的歷史多元文化背景。 它涵蓋了各種果阿身份群體，並在英語和天城文孔卡尼語中包含了313對最小對。 然後，我們在這個基準上評估了五個多語言編碼器模型中的刻板印象偏見。 我們發現在孔卡尼語中的得分接近隨機，反映出一般多語言模型的語言能力不足，以及印度語言模型缺乏果阿文化能力。 用英語查詢時，具有更強印度語言覆蓋的模型對全印度群體顯示出比超地方果阿群體更高的偏見。 這表明英語信號反映的是全印度預訓練聯繫，而非真正的果阿文化知識。 我們的發現突顯了低資源多語言自然語言處理評估在超地方社區身份方面的關鍵缺口。
2606.15191.author: Michelle Barbosa et.al.
2606.15191.authors: Michelle Barbosa, Sebastian Padó, Franziska Weeber
